review_title: Android Studio, my stepping stone to app development.
description: Incredible performance, and user friendly. I've been using Android Studio for a while and have completed few projects written in Kotlin and Flutter language. Proud moment for me, as my first Android App was built using Android Studio and the journey I had was truly challenging but with this, it was a lot more easier.
author: Dawn S.
pros: The efficiency and the flow is the main key that raises the bars of Android Studio. Special appreciation for the User Interface.
cons: Often faced errors while setting up the on device simulator to test the apps other that it is a game changer.

description: I got promoted thanks to android studio. It's UI and UX really easy to use. And you can find much resources for it. Just search it.
author: Ali S.
pros: First of all i love the idea game development. It's giving a chance to develop games. It's not specially build for it but you can develop a game. I'm using this software almost for 10 years. I've started from java. Now i can also develop flutter. You can build almost anything for mobile. It's have virtual devices so you dont have to worry about to have physical device. Plus if you have a physical device and if you connected to it just once you can continue build with wi-fi. It was the first time that i see it with android studio. It's really a masterpiece for android development.
cons: Well i really dislike latest Logcat. Android studio should colorize old LogCat and it's all set.

review_title: Good IDE for mobile app. Great Emulator.
description: Android Studio is a great tool for mobile developers. White the IDE is not easy to use and very resource heavy, the emulator alone is worth the install for easier android app testing and debugging.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Powerful IDE focused on android development. It is feature packed with essential functions such as built-in emulator which is the most useful feature for my mobile app developments.
cons: Very resource heavy program, even on high-end PC I'm currently using, I'm, still experiencing lags and freezes. It is also not very easy to learn and may be more time consuming to develop an android app.

description: I was completely frustrated and done. I soon switched back to my Apple products.
author: Monica M.
pros: I can’t say I did. I am an Apple user at heart, so Android anything just drives me nananas.
cons: The fact that it is an Android. I mean honestly I can’t find anything better than Apple!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: The only tool for android dev
description: It's literally the only tool that does what I need for app development. In particular, I use it to figure out which selectors actually work for certain tasks in appium
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It was the only reliable way for me to run an app through a suite of tests. The appium connector was mostly used in tandem with android studio to achieve this. Really convenient.
cons: The amount of battery that android studio consumes (on my macbook pro m4) is insane. I wish it was a little bit more efficient.

description: I feel this IDE help me start and finish any android app, it has many fractures and tools others IDE doesn't
author: Samuel Isaí R.
pros: I love all cool tools android studio provides, that renders my design in kotlin and xml, and that I can drag and drop elements to the UI I'm developing.Also the new IDE UI is cleaner and beautiful
cons: Shortcuts are not intuitive, so it would be nice to set VSCode shortcuts faster, also android studio is not exactly a low size IDE, so it takes a lot of system resources

review_title: Best IDE for android and flutter projects
description: I like writing code with smart code suggesting, a lot of pluggings that helps write code fastest and a great interface
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easily integration with android virtual devices and 100% compatibility with flutter projects
cons: Android virtual devices sometimes don't run as expected

review_title: The go-to solution for building Android applications
description: My overall experience with Android Studio is really good. IntelliJ has done an amazing job with this IDE. I've been using it for more than 10 years now and have successfully developed more than 5 applications on it. I wish it was even simpler to use with a less stiff learning curve and I also wish it was a bit lighter in terms of resource usage.
author: Vasilis K.
pros: I love the user interface. IntelliJ has done a great job. It also makes it really easy to spin up virtual devices with the os version of your choice.
cons: Very recently I updated Android Studio to the latest version and it introduced many issues to my Flutter project. An incompatibility of Java and Grandle came up and I'm still trying to resolve it.

description: Very good; it is very helpful for Android app development and it is very feature-rich to support development.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It has a clean interface and is well organized. It's good for developing android apps and has a good emulator as well.
cons: It can be difficult to get used to if coming from a different IDE, since it has extra features specific to android development and a different layout.

review_title: Best IDE for creating Android APKs
description: Positive experience. Builds the Android app including the manifest ready for submission to the Play store.
author: Eric C.
pros: De-facto standard for creating Android applications. Does as promised!
cons: We used a hybrid system where our frontend is rendered with React in the Android application. Didn't integrate completely with Android Studio and still required some manual steps during the build.

review_title: Android Studio... Worth taking a look at.
description: I'm still working to figure this out.. because I love learning new things
author: James W.
pros: I am an amateur android developer and I love trying to figure new things out
cons: It's not very user (noob) friendly haven't really finished anything I've started yet.

description: Very positive. It could have been much bad so cant complain, wish some performance improvement.
author: Hammad A.
pros: I use it the most and it is very dependable. I love the Git window and I always prefer it over other desktop tools like GITHub and SourceTree.
cons: Some tools are impossible to use on windows, Like the app inspector works for the first couple of minutes when inspecting network but it slows the whole system down way too much, have to detach the inspector to get the Speed back. AVD is also not very dependable and very slow on windows. On mac though its much better. Also I wish some more and easily available shortcuts for boilerplate would have been nice.

description: It's an inexpensive and easy to use software. This software will definitely add business value to an organization, team, or individual that uses it to create android applications. I enjoy using It and Advice others to try it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Security and Bugs are not a major concern when using this software because there is often updates that keep it secure and stable. I like How easy it is to integrate this tool with other IDE's like Visual Studio, Pycharm, and Netbeans. I also like the fact that Editing Code in Android studio is very easy because a developer can view their code just by clicking the Syntax view button that is located in the top-right corner of the View.
cons: The Software is very resource heavy. I advice a developer to have at least 8GB of RAM and at least 4 cores to use this application stably. I also don't like how the application is sometimes slow to respond when its used while connected to the internet.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: 100+ plugins, fast due to many templetes, stand alone java applications
cons: consume a lot of resources of the cpu, difficult to install requres expert

review_title: Android Studio is such an awseome tool
description: I have been using Android Studio for almost 2 years now and it has been practically the only tool I have used for developing Android apps, it has layout edition, compiling, debugging, you can query local DBs directly from the tool, and a lot more.
author: José Luis C.
pros: This is such a powerful and yet an easy to use tool for developing Android applications, at the beginning it may be a bit intimidating the navigation and the different options it has but as you get more and more used to it you'll see the reason why. That's basically all you'll ever need from writing your first line of code to deployment of the app.
cons: Not any that I can identify, probably I was not able to see how to develop some Node.js functions I needed to deploy in Firebase and I had to use another tool for it. But that was just a one time issue.

description: The overall experience was good . It needs more ram.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: 1. The UI is very nice 2. So much plugins to choose from 3. Loved the material theme 4. Support for flutter tools , like run , test , dev tools 5. Best for app development
cons: 1. It needs more storage and ram to work 2. The startup time is more compare to other 3. It takes time to analyze the project

review_title: very good for creating android application
description: the tutorial that Android studio will have to propose us must be in a big number of language has to know English French German Chinese Arabic and Russian
author: eric lionel Z.
pros: integrates several languages the fact of being able to preview the work done is very good it allows to see the defects of its application
cons: the defect of this software is that I don't find easily a good tutorial I think they have to work on this issue also androide studio presents too many options that are not easy to understand

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It supports to create android emulator easily. Able to use it cross OSs on desktop.
cons: I started to use it for Flutter development and emulating Android device but for a while, there is an app better than Android Studio as a development environment. Currently I'm using it just for Android Virtual Devices.

author: João Q.
pros: The built-in emulator . Ability to debug native apps
cons: The built-in emulator can be slow and unreliable.

description: Very easy to develop application for my business.
author: Mohit S.
pros: The most important features of Android Studio are good ui and easy to understand basic app development.
cons: Some dependency must improved in Android Studio.

author: Yusril A.
pros: Integrated with google service like firebae, gcp, and more
cons: Improve on memory management, emulator can smooth run

description: Android Studio makes it easy to create mobile applications for all platforms
author: Denis A.
pros: I like the Android studio because you can create your own applications. Most of the options are accessible with a simple mouse click.
cons: when working on Android Studio with other tools It uses a lot of RAM and CPU.

review_title: The best tool to get started in Android mobile app development
description: I have developed some applications related to the consumption of Google services, it has so much flexibility and compatibility with other tools, which allows developing native android applications with great ease, an intuitive interface environment allows developing easily without the need to know code based on the styling of the components.
author: Eutimio A.
pros: This IDE allows you to use default Android application templates that will allow you to easily develop an application in just a few programming sessions. Similarly, if your development is based on Java, you will have no problem in implementing applications.
cons: It has so many functionalities that it will be tedious to understand how the tool works 100%.

review_title: Best for Android Development but not iOS native applications
description: I had good experience with Android studio developing a marketplace for repairs. The service would provide different contacts for repairs of house products like kitchen, appliances, furniture, etc.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Android apps are very responsive & development is pretty smooth with features loaded.Can make it work across all versions of Android availableEasy to debug Android errorsNot very huge learning curve
cons: iOS native apps are not something to be developed on Android studio. Personally, I feel it could be easier to manage configurations.Android studio does not run smoothly on low-end platforms

review_title: Easy create Android apps from scratch
description: Overall great experience using Android Studio
author: Jose J.
pros: Effective debugging features, great set of tools and plugins, easy to use and quick learning, open source and user friendly interface.
cons: Android studio consumes more RAM and it lags sometimes here and there
